description: Replace ReLU² MLP with parameter-balanced SwiGLU.
reasoning: Diagnosis: Cross-layer capacity is decent, but the current relu² MLP is an outdated activation that wastes parameters and limits representation power, especially for small models where every bit matters. Modern small-scale LMs (LLaMA, Gemma, Mistral) all replaced ReLU with SwiGLU which gives a noticeably lower | Hypothesis: Switch the feed-forward block to SwiGLU while keeping the overall parameter count constant (choose hidden = 2/3·mlp_mult·d and pack gate+up into one Linear). This increases multiplicative expressivity and usually yields ~0.5–1.0 ppl point improvements which should translate to ~0.003–0.006 BPB on Fi | Expected: -0.004
